questions.question: The graph of y = sin(x) is shifted 3 units to the right and 2 units up. Write the equation of the transformed function.
questions.explanation: Horizontal shifts appear inside the function: shift right by 3 means replace x with (x - 3). Vertical shifts are added outside: shift up by 2 means add 2 to the output.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A = 45°, angle B = 60°, and side a = 10. Find side b using the Law of Sines. Round to the nearest tenth.
What is the period of the function y = cos(2x)?
questions.explanation: For y = cos(bx), the period is 2π/|b|. Here b = 2, so period = 2π/2 = π.

questions.question: In triangle PQR, p = 5, q = 7, and angle R = 120°. Find side r using the Law of Cosines.
The amplitude of y = -3sin(x) is how much greater than the amplitude of y = sin(x/2)?
questions.explanation: Amplitude of y = -3sin(x) is |-3| = 3. Amplitude of y = sin(x/2) is |1| = 1. The difference is 3 - 1 = 2.

questions.question: In triangle DEF, side d = 8, side e = 6, and side f = 7. Which angle is largest, and why?
questions.answer: Angle D is largest because the largest angle is opposite the longest side.
questions.explanation: In any triangle, the largest angle is opposite the largest side. Side d = 8 is the longest side, so angle D (opposite to d) is the largest.
